properties.name: Dangote Refinery
properties.description: Africa's largest single-train refinery. 650,000 bpd capacity. Produces petrol, diesel, jet fuel, and polypropylene. Expected to significantly reduce Nigeria's dependence on imported refined products.

properties.name: Port Harcourt Refinery
properties.description: Two plants (60,000 + 150,000 bpd). Currently undergoing $1.5B rehabilitation by Tecnimont SpA. Nigeria's largest government-owned refinery.

properties.name: Warri Refinery
properties.description: Built 1978, 125,000 bpd capacity. Produces petrol, kerosene, diesel. Has operated below capacity for years. Rehabilitation ongoing.

properties.name: Kaduna Refinery
properties.description: Nigeria's only inland refinery, 110,000 bpd. Strategically serves northern Nigeria. Receives crude via pipeline from Niger Delta. Being rehabilitated.
